title: Body-safe materials: the complete visual guide

hook_slide.headline: Is your 'safe' toy actually harming you?
hook_slide.subline: Discover the truth about body-safe materials before your next intimate experience.
hook_slide.image_concept: Dark, warm, textured background with subtle, abstract light play, evoking curiosity and safety.

education_slides.headline: Platinum-Cured Silicone: The Gold Standard
education_slides.body: Look for 100% platinum-cured silicone. It's non-porous, hypoallergenic, and resistant to bacteria, making it ideal for internal use.
education_slides.image_concept: Smooth, clean, minimalist texture representing high-quality silicone, perhaps with a subtle sheen.

education_slides.headline: Borosilicate Glass: Elegant & Hygienic
education_slides.body: Medical-grade borosilicate glass is non-porous, easy to clean, and doesn't harbor bacteria. It's a beautiful, body-safe choice for many.
education_slides.image_concept: Translucent, clean, elegant texture, perhaps with a soft, diffused light passing through.

education_slides.headline: Surgical Stainless Steel: Durable & Safe
education_slides.body: Often used in medical implants, surgical stainless steel is non-porous, hypoallergenic, and incredibly durable. Easy to sterilize for peace of mind.
education_slides.image_concept: Polished, sleek, metallic texture, reflecting soft light, conveying strength and cleanliness.

education_slides.headline: ABS Plastic: When Battery-Operated
education_slides.body: High-quality ABS plastic is a hard, non-porous material often found in external vibrators. Ensure it's clearly labeled 'body-safe' and free of phthalates.
education_slides.image_concept: Smooth, opaque, matte plastic texture, indicating a solid, robust material.

education_slides.headline: Hard Plastics: Read Labels Carefully
education_slides.body: Polycarbonate (PC) and polypropylene (PP) can be body-safe for external use if virgin plastic and phthalate-free. Avoid porous, soft plastics.
education_slides.image_concept: Slightly more textured, less refined plastic appearance, with a subtle overlay hinting at caution or a question mark.

education_slides.headline: Porous Materials: A Hidden Danger
education_slides.body: Jelly, PVC, and TPR are often porous, meaning they can harbor bacteria and leach harmful chemicals. Avoid these materials for any intimate contact.
education_slides.image_concept: Soft, slightly sticky, ambiguous texture, perhaps with a subtle 'X' or warning symbol integrated into the design.

education_slides.headline: Understanding Porosity & Phthalates
education_slides.body: Porous materials can't be fully cleaned, trapping bacteria. Phthalates are chemicals found in many plastics that can disrupt hormones. Always check labels!
education_slides.image_concept: A visual metaphor for absorption vs. non-absorption, or a simplified chemical structure icon subtly in the background, signaling health implications.

framework_slide.title: Your Body-Safe Materials Checklist
items: 💡 Always clean thoroughly before & after use!
framework_slide.image_concept: Clean, organized layout with clear checkmarks and 'X' symbols, on a soft, inviting background.

cta_slide.headline: Curated by Dark Fantasy
cta_slide.image_concept: Minimal Dark Fantasy logo, elegant and centered, on a warm, inviting background with subtle texture.

caption: Ever wondered what makes a material truly 'body-safe'? 🤔 Dive into our complete guide to ensure your intimate experiences are always healthy and enjoyable. Protect your wellness!
